reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify the political figure who relaunched his career in Scotland before the 1880 General Election.
2. Determine which party he led during that campaign and return the party name.
original_final_question: When William Gladstone successfully relaunched his political career in Scotland in advance of the 1880 General Election, which party did he lead? Write your answer as [ANSWER].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: Which British Liberal statesman relaunched his political career in Scotland before the 1880 General Election? Evidence: William Ewart Gladstone: William Ewart Gladstone, ( ; 29 December 1809 – 19 May 1898) was a British Liberal statesman and earlier Conservative politician. In a career lasting over 60 years, he served as Prime Minister four separate times (1868–74, 1880–85, February–July 1886 and 1892–94), more than any other person, and served as Chancellor of the Exchequer four times. -> William Ewart Gladstone

Node 2: Which party did William Ewart Gladstone lead during the Midlothian campaign? Evidence: Midlothian campaign: The Midlothian campaign of 1878–80 was a series of foreign policy speeches given by William Ewart Gladstone, leader of Britain's Liberal Party. -> Liberal Party
